title: Tracking Cost-Adjusted Accuracy
concept: Calculating task success rates normalized by the token cost and execution time of the run.

exercises.prompt: What is one way to gather feedback about AI performance?
exercises.explain: User-Satisfaction Surveys are a structured way to collect feedback on how well an AI is performing according to user preferences.

cards.title: What is Cost-Adjusted Accuracy?
cards.body: Cost-Adjusted Accuracy is a way to measure how successful a task is by taking into account both the costs and time it took to complete the task.

cards.title: Defining the Concept
cards.body: We calculate Cost-Adjusted Accuracy by combining the number of successful tasks with the total token cost and execution time. This helps give a clearer picture of efficiency.

cards.title: Imagine a Sports Game
cards.body: Think of it like scoring points in a basketball game while also considering how much time you played and how many shots you took. You want to know how efficiently your team scored compared to the effort put in.

cards.title: Calculating an Example
cards.body: If an AI successfully completes 80 tasks with a token cost of 200 and took 40 minutes, the cost-adjusted accuracy helps us see how well it performed given these constraints.

cards.title: Myth: Any Success is Enough
cards.body: Some might think just completing tasks is all that matters. However, without considering costs or time, you can't truly measure the effectiveness of a task.

cards.title: Visualizing Cost-Adjusted Accuracy
cards.body: Here's how success, token cost, and time interact:

exercises.prompt: What does Cost-Adjusted Accuracy help us understand?

options.text: The number of tasks without context

exercises.explain: Cost-Adjusted Accuracy helps us understand how efficiently tasks are completed by relating success to the resources and time used.

exercises.prompt: Cost-Adjusted Accuracy considers only the number of successful tasks.
exercises.explain: False, because Cost-Adjusted Accuracy also includes token costs and execution time to provide a fuller picture of efficiency.

exercises.prompt: To find Cost-Adjusted Accuracy, we look at successful tasks versus their ____ and execution time.
exercises.sentence: To find Cost-Adjusted Accuracy, we look at successful tasks versus their token cost and execution time.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'token cost' because we must account for the resources spent completing the tasks.

exercises.prompt: Which calculation helps determine Cost-Adjusted Accuracy?
exercises.explain: The correct calculation is Successful Tasks divided by the total of Token Cost and Execution Time to assess efficiency.

exercises.prompt: If an AI completes 100 successful tasks, using 300 tokens and taking 60 minutes, what is the Cost-Adjusted Accuracy?
exercises.explain: The correct calculation is 100 successful tasks divided by the total cost (300 tokens + 60 minutes = 360) to get the efficiency ratio of completion.
